description: A designed vertical anti-scam warning infographic. The top header is a dark red banner with bold white text 'BEWARE OF SCAMS and always keep these tips in mind:' accompanied by a large red exclamation-point graphic on the right. Below are three stacked tips, each with a small illustrative icon on the left and explanatory text on the right: (1) a computer/monitor icon showing an email with a '.gov' address - 'Make sure emails are sent from addresses ending in .gov'; (2) an illustration of a person on a phone with a red X - 'USCIS will never ask for money or payment over the phone'; (3) a browser/URL icon - 'Always preview a link and check that a URL ends in .gov before clicking on it.' Bottom-left shows the DHS seal and 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark. Clean white background with red/dark accents in standard government PSA style. Messaging is consumer-protection guidance warning immigrants about fraud and impersonation scams. Tweet describes scammers calling applicants to demand payment by phone.
notable_text: BEWARE OF SCAMS and always keep these tips in mind; USCIS will never ask for money or payment over the phone; Always preview a link and check that a URL ends in .gov
